name: Zero Trust Security Model
description: A security strategy that assumes breach and verifies every request regardless of network location.
key_facts: Zero Trust principles: Verify explicitly, Use least privilege access, Assume breach
Traditional perimeter security assumes everything inside the network is trusted; Zero Trust does not
Every access request is authenticated, authorized, and encrypted regardless of source location
Zero Trust applies to users, devices, applications, and data—not just the network
Microsoft's Zero Trust framework covers six pillars: Identities, Devices, Apps, Data, Infrastructure, Network
common_misconceptions: Zero Trust is not a single product; it is a security strategy implemented across multiple services
Implementing Zero Trust does not require rebuilding from scratch; it can be adopted incrementally

name: Encryption, Hashing, and Cryptography Basics
description: Foundational cryptographic concepts used to protect data at rest and in transit in Microsoft security services.
key_facts: Symmetric encryption uses the same key for encryption and decryption; fast but requires secure key exchange
Asymmetric encryption uses a public key to encrypt and private key to decrypt; used in TLS and digital signatures
Hashing is a one-way function that produces a fixed-length digest; used for verifying data integrity
Digital certificates use asymmetric keys to prove identity; managed by Certificate Authorities (CAs)
Azure Key Vault stores encryption keys, secrets, and certificates with HSM backing
common_misconceptions: Hashing is not encryption; hashes cannot be reversed to obtain the original data
Encryption at rest and in transit require different key management strategies

name: Authentication vs Authorization
description: Two fundamental identity security concepts that control who can access resources and what they can do.
key_facts: Authentication verifies who you are; authorization determines what you can access
Multi-factor Authentication (MFA) requires two or more verification factors: something you know, have, or are
Single Sign-On (SSO) allows one login to access multiple applications without re-authenticating
OAuth 2.0 is an authorization framework; OpenID Connect is an identity layer on top of OAuth 2.0
Passwordless authentication uses biometrics, FIDO2 keys, or authenticator apps instead of passwords
common_misconceptions: Authentication and authorization are sequential; authentication always happens before authorization
SSO does not reduce security when properly implemented with MFA and Conditional Access

name: Microsoft Entra ID
description: Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service, the foundation for Zero Trust security.
key_facts: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-native identity provider
Entra ID supports Azure, Microsoft 365, and thousands of SaaS app integrations via SSO
Tenants are dedicated Entra ID instances; each organization gets its own tenant
Hybrid identity synchronizes on-premises AD to Entra ID using Entra Connect
Entra ID P1 and P2 licenses unlock Conditional Access, PIM, and Identity Protection features
common_misconceptions: Entra ID is not Windows Active Directory; it uses cloud protocols like SAML, OAuth, and OIDC
Free tier Entra ID includes basic SSO and MFA; premium features require P1 or P2 license

name: Microsoft Entra Conditional Access
description: Policy-based access control that evaluates signals to grant, limit, or block access to resources.
key_facts: Conditional Access policies evaluate signals: user/group, device, location, application, and risk
Policies can require MFA, compliant device, specific location, or block access entirely
Named locations define trusted IP ranges or countries for Conditional Access evaluation
Conditional Access requires Entra ID P1 license at minimum
Report-only mode tests policies without enforcing them to understand impact before deployment
common_misconceptions: Conditional Access is not just about MFA; it can enforce device compliance, app restrictions, and more
Conditional Access policies apply to the authentication event, not individual resource requests

name: Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management
description: A service that provides just-in-time privileged access to Azure AD and Azure resources with approval workflows.
key_facts: PIM enables just-in-time (JIT) privileged access so elevated roles are active only when needed
Eligible assignments require activation before use; active assignments are always active
PIM requires approval, justification, and MFA for role activation to reduce standing privilege risk
PIM provides access reviews to regularly certify who should have privileged roles
PIM requires Entra ID P2 license
common_misconceptions: PIM is not just for Azure AD roles; it also manages Azure resource roles like Owner and Contributor
Eligible roles in PIM do not automatically activate; users must explicitly activate and provide justification

name: Microsoft Defender Security Solutions
description: Microsoft's family of security products providing threat protection across endpoints, identity, cloud, and data.
key_facts: Microsoft Defender for Cloud secures Azure, on-premises, and multi-cloud workloads with security posture management
Microsoft Defender XDR (formerly 365 Defender) protects endpoints, email, identities, and cloud apps
Defender for Endpoint protects Windows, macOS, Linux, and mobile devices with EDR capabilities
Defender for Identity monitors on-premises Active Directory for suspicious activities
Secure Score measures and improves security posture; higher score means better configuration
common_misconceptions: Microsoft Defender for Cloud and Microsoft Defender for Endpoint are different products serving different scopes
Secure Score improvement does not guarantee no breaches; it measures configuration, not threat prevention

name: Microsoft Sentinel: SIEM and SOAR
description: Cloud-native security information and event management (SIEM) and security orchestration automated response (SOAR).
key_facts: Microsoft Sentinel collects security data at cloud scale from users, devices, applications, and infrastructure
Sentinel uses AI and analytics to detect, investigate, and respond to threats automatically
Workbooks provide visual dashboards for monitoring and investigating security incidents
Playbooks automate response to alerts using Azure Logic Apps workflows
Data connectors integrate Sentinel with Microsoft 365, Azure, and third-party security products
common_misconceptions: Sentinel is a cloud-native SIEM; it does not require on-premises infrastructure unlike traditional SIEMs
Playbooks in Sentinel automate responses but still require human review for sensitive actions

name: Azure Key Vault and Secret Management
description: Managed service for securely storing and accessing secrets, encryption keys, and certificates.
key_facts: Azure Key Vault stores secrets (passwords, connection strings), keys, and certificates
Keys in Key Vault can be hardware-protected using HSMs (Hardware Security Modules)
Access to Key Vault is controlled via Azure RBAC and Key Vault access policies
Key rotation can be automated for Azure services to reduce secret exposure risk
Managed identities eliminate the need for credentials by giving Azure services an identity for Key Vault access
common_misconceptions: Key Vault does not manage all secrets in Azure; developers must explicitly use it in their applications
Deleting a Key Vault or secret does not immediately destroy data; soft-delete and purge protection are available

name: Microsoft Purview Compliance Solutions
description: Microsoft's unified data governance and compliance platform for managing data across the organization.
key_facts: Microsoft Purview provides data governance, risk management, and compliance solutions
Information Protection classifies and labels sensitive data using sensitivity labels
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) prevents accidental sharing of sensitive information via policies
Compliance Manager helps assess compliance posture and provides improvement actions
eDiscovery and Audit enable legal hold and investigation of user activities
common_misconceptions: Purview is not just for Microsoft 365; it can govern data across Azure, on-premises, and multi-cloud
Sensitivity labels and retention labels are different; sensitivity labels protect data, retention labels manage lifecycle

name: Regulatory Compliance and Azure Compliance
description: Key compliance frameworks and how Microsoft Azure meets regulatory requirements for security and privacy.
key_facts: GDPR: EU regulation for protecting personal data of EU citizens; organizations must report breaches within 72 hours
ISO 27001: International standard for information security management systems
SOC 1, 2, 3: Reports from auditors on a service organization's controls; SOC 2 covers security
Azure Trust Center and Service Trust Portal provide compliance documentation and audit reports
Compliance Manager in Purview maps controls to regulatory frameworks and scores your compliance posture
common_misconceptions: Using Azure does not automatically make your application GDPR-compliant; you must also configure your own controls
ISO 27001 certification of Azure does not transfer to your applications hosted on Azure

name: Azure Network Security Solutions
description: Security services that protect Azure network infrastructure from threats and unauthorized access.
key_facts: Network Security Groups (NSGs) filter inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources at subnet or NIC level
Azure Firewall is a managed, stateful firewall with FQDN filtering and threat intelligence integration
Azure DDoS Protection Standard provides enhanced mitigation for DDoS attacks on Azure resources
Azure Web Application Firewall (WAF) protects web apps from OWASP top 10 vulnerabilities
Azure Bastion provides secure RDP/SSH access to VMs without exposing public IP addresses
common_misconceptions: NSGs are stateful; if inbound traffic is allowed, response outbound traffic is automatically allowed
Azure Firewall and NSGs serve different purposes and are often used together for layered security

name: Microsoft Entra ID Governance
description: Tools and processes for managing the identity lifecycle and ensuring appropriate access throughout an organization.
key_facts: Entitlement Management automates access request, assignment, and review workflows for groups and apps
Access Reviews periodically certify that users still need their assigned access
Lifecycle Workflows automate identity tasks at employee joiners, movers, and leavers events
Identity Governance helps enforce least privilege by regularly reviewing and removing unnecessary access
Terms of Use requires users to accept policy statements before accessing resources
common_misconceptions: Access Reviews must be configured; they do not automatically detect and remove stale access
Entitlement Management is available only with Entra ID Governance license, not the standard P2

name: Microsoft 365 Security and Information Protection
description: Security capabilities within Microsoft 365 for protecting users, devices, apps, and data.
key_facts: Microsoft Defender for Office 365 protects against email threats like phishing and malware
Sensitivity labels in Microsoft 365 classify and protect documents and emails
Microsoft Intune manages and enforces compliance policies on mobile and desktop devices
Microsoft 365 Compliance Center (now Purview portal) is the hub for compliance tools
Insider Risk Management detects risky user activities that could lead to data leakage
common_misconceptions: Defender for Office 365 protection applies at email arrival; it does not monitor all user file activity
Device compliance policies in Intune only enforce; Conditional Access uses compliance state to grant access

name: Role-Based Access Control and Identity Principles
description: Access control models that govern how permissions are assigned and enforced in Microsoft security solutions.
key_facts: RBAC assigns permissions to roles and then assigns roles to users, rather than giving permissions directly
Principle of Least Privilege: grant only the minimum access necessary to perform a function
Separation of Duties: no single person should have control of all stages of a critical process
Azure built-in roles: Owner (full), Contributor (all except assign), Reader (view only)
Custom roles can be created when built-in roles do not meet specific least-privilege requirements
common_misconceptions: RBAC in Azure is about Azure resource permissions; Entra ID roles are separate and manage directory permissions
Owner role in Azure does not grant access to read Entra ID users; those require separate Entra ID roles
